name: Cohen and Cohen
description_short: Denver-based bankruptcy law firm offering Chapter 7, Chapter 13, business bankruptcy, and related debt services for individuals and businesses.
description_long:
Cohen Law Denver is a Colorado-based bankruptcy and debt relief law firm founded and led by Rob Cohen, with a team that includes Will Evans (practicing since 1985, 20+ years bankruptcy focus), Gary Brown (bankruptcy specialist with family law and mediation experience), and other licensed attorneys. The firm positions itself as a full-service legal institution handling both individual consumer bankruptcy and business insolvency matters, operating from Denver with a focus on personalized legal representation.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services including Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing and qualification assistance, Chapter 13 bankruptcy planning, business bankruptcy (Chapter 11 and Subchapter V), bankruptcy litigation, adversary proceedings, and contested matters. Beyond traditional bankruptcy, they provide debt settlement consultation (comparing it against bankruptcy alternatives), mediation services (both facilitative and evaluative), and specialized guidance on topics like student loan discharge, tax debt in bankruptcy, foreclosure prevention, and Colorado bankruptcy exemptions.

According to client testimonials, Cohen Law Denver distinguishes itself through thorough case preparation, attention to detail, and personalized consultation rather than formulaic intake processes. The firm explicitly acknowledges pricing differences between attorneys and offers clients the option to work with associates if cost is a primary concern. Rob Cohen's active litigation practice and the team's experience with related legal areas (family law, appeals, negotiation) suggest a broader legal competency than bankruptcy alone.

The firm operates a legitimate law practice with a professional website, client portal, payment processing, and published team bios. However, as a law firm rather than a financial services company, Cohen Law Denver is appropriate for consumers who have already determined bankruptcy is necessary or need legal guidance on debt relief options—not for those seeking to avoid bankruptcy or rebuild credit independently.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Team includes attorneys with 20+ years of bankruptcy experience (Will Evans practicing since 1985)
Offers free initial consultations to assess whether bankruptcy or alternative debt relief is appropriate
Provides both individual consumer bankruptcy (Chapter 7, 13) and business bankruptcy services (Chapter 11, Subchapter V)
Specializes in complex bankruptcy topics like student loan discharge, tax debt, foreclosure prevention, and adversary proceedings
Client testimonial highlights thorough case preparation and attention to detail that led to early hearing approval
Offers alternative services including debt settlement, mediation, and comparative analysis (bankruptcy vs. debt settlement vs. consolidation)
Rob Cohen maintains active litigation practice to stay current with legal developments and improve firm representation
cons: Law firm pricing is explicitly positioned as premium compared to associates; no published fee structure for transparency
Primary service (bankruptcy filing) requires consumers to already be in financial distress; not suitable for credit building or prevention
Limited online information about typical outcomes, average fees, or case statistics beyond one testimonial
Website does not clearly specify geographic service area beyond Denver/Colorado or whether they handle cases outside Colorado
No mention of payment plans, flat fees, or flexible retainer arrangements for cost-conscious consumers
best_for: Colorado consumers considering Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy who want legal representation from experienced attorneys
Small business owners facing insolvency and needing Chapter 11 or Subchapter V bankruptcy guidance
Individuals with complex bankruptcy situations (student loan discharge, tax debt, foreclosure, adversary proceedings)
Consumers uncertain whether bankruptcy or debt settlement is the right approach and seeking professional legal evaluation
diagnosis: Cohen Law Denver is best for Colorado-based individuals and businesses who have determined bankruptcy is necessary and need experienced legal representation through the filing and discharge process. The main caveat is that this is a premium law firm positioned above budget options, and consumers should verify current fee structure, Colorado-only service limitations, and whether their specific bankruptcy chapter complexity justifies higher representation costs.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing and qualification assessment
Contested matters resolution in bankruptcy cases
Student loan discharge in bankruptcy consultation
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
